commands.repair_reason: Manual review is needed to choose between this PR and the open alternative and to accept the compatibility tradeoff; there is no narrow automation repair outstanding.; Cleared: The diff only changes internal plugin loader compatibility logic and focused tests; no concrete secret-handling, dependency, CI, or supply-chain concern was found. (sha=60ab3dd0ca21ee5771cdc78268e2761ac6ae79f7)
commands.review_summary:
The PR extends plugin-loader active-registry compatibility so dispatch can reuse a gateway-startup registry whose cache key differs only by startup-only built-artifact preference, with focused regression tests.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection shows gateway startup uses gateway-bindable plus prefer-built options while dispatch uses gateway-bindable options without the startup-only preference, and the PR supplies live runtime-loader output for that shape.
commands.review_followup: Manual review is needed to choose between this PR and the open alternative and to accept the compatibility tradeoff; there is no narrow automation repair outstanding.

target.title: perf(plugins): reuse compatible gateway startup registry on dispatch
target.body:
## Summary

- Reuse an active gateway-startup registry when dispatch load options are compatible but omit the startup-only built-artifact preference.
- Keep loader cache compatibility checks for workspace, plugin scope, config, gateway methods, and runtime mode so changed config still forces a fresh load.
- Add regression coverage for loader compatibility and standalone dispatch reuse.

Fixes #80682
Alternative to #80691.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Dispatch-time standalone runtime ensure should reuse a compatible gateway-startup registry instead of reloading when dispatch already passes gateway-bindable runtime options and only the startup-only built-artifact preference differs.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source checkout from this PR branch, Node 22.22.2 on Linux, runtime loader path with an active gateway-bindable registry.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `timeout 30s node --import tsx/esm --input-type=module` with a local runtime-loader probe.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):

  ```text
  Real behavior proof for PR #84324
  Environment: local OpenClaw source checkout, Node 22.22.2, runtime loader path
  Dispatch options mode: gateway-bindable
  Startup registry mode: gateway-bindable
  Startup cache key preserved: true
  Dispatch ensure returned active registry: true
  Active registry unchanged: true
  Required plugin present: true
  ```

- Observed result after fix: The dispatch-time ensure call with gateway-bindable runtime options returned the already-active startup registry and left the active registry unchanged.
- What was not tested: Full gateway/channel E2E was not run locally; focused proof covers the runtime-loader reuse path, and regression tests cover compatibility plus changed-config reload.

## Verification

- pnpm test src/plugins/loader.runtime-registry.test.ts src/plugins/runtime/standalone-runtime-registry-loader.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose
- pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/plugins/loader.ts src/plugins/loader.runtime-registry.test.ts src/plugins/runtime/standalone-runtime-registry-loader.test.ts
- git diff --check
target.author: ai-hpc

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
